mapped_only.reason: XML parser cases require explicit fixture/schema support before active import.
Multipart parser error and file collection branches exceed the current minimal multipart smoke.
Converter tooling is build-runtime support, not a portable rule case.
Full-byte, NUL, and invalid-encoding urlDecode branches are mapped only; active smoke imports only source-confirmed text-safe Test+Case -> Test Case.
NUL, nbsp, non-ASCII, and invalid entity branches are mapped only; active smoke imports only the text-safe <> -> <> fragment.
Long phrase-list and no-match branches are mapped only; active smoke imports the source-confirmed param abc/input abcdefghi match.
Negative word-boundary branches are mapped only; active smoke imports the source-confirmed param abc/input abc def ghi match.
Invalid config tests require config-test expected-failure assertions rather than HTTP smokes.
Large-response phase-4 regression needs dedicated large fixture/log-leak coverage before active import.
Response-body blocking remains xfail/mapped-only and is not promoted without stable real HTTP blocking semantics.

blocked.reason: HTTP/2 is outside the current HTTP/1.1 smoke harness.
HTTP/2 is outside the current HTTP/1.1 smoke harness.

xfail.reason: Dedicated three-repeat Phase 9 probe stayed xfail/mapped-only: Apache and NGINX both returned HTTP 200 instead of stable HTTP 403. PR #377 source is applied to adapter-owned NGINX files, but RESPONSE_BODY is not promoted without stable real HTTP behavior.
Local Apache and NGINX runs observed empty audit logs, but GitHub Actions reported a non-empty audit log; it is not counted as stable common PASS until both environments agree.
Strict mode may abort the connection after headers are sent; current smoke schema asserts stable HTTP status and keeps this as NGINX-only xfail evidence.
